title: Travel Occurrences — pre-China mishaps catalog
html:
TL;DR

A running list, started Aug 14 2024 (early china chapter) and modified all the way to Dec 17 2024, where Yahir banks his best/worst travel anecdotes from the years before shanghai. Reads like raw material for a future memoir, podcast, or travel content creator reel. Ends with a short supplement stack inventory grafted on at the bottom.

The catalog

  • Getting lost in northern vietnam and meeting vu van ba
  • Being assaulted by an elderly Indian man in varanasi
  • Accidentally going off-trail at petra with emilio and having to scale down a mountain
  • Finessing the conventional machu picchu route into a 25-mile walk
  • Wrong-highway in vietnam, met berkoo
  • Checking into the nagpur police station with yoav
  • Bringing yoav to an Indian hospital — saw a roaming monkey and a near-dead man
  • 20+ hour lima→cusco bus ride that had him dissociating
  • Leaving Dutch Anna, then reuniting on a random island in vietnam
  • The gnarliest food poisoning of his life in the white desert egypt
  • Essentially moneyless between jordan and israel thanks to a rogue credit union
  • 7-day trek with a "virgin 34 year old"

Why it matters in the graph

  • This is Yahir's story bank — the raw inputs for the "could be a great… travel content creator" line in 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure / career archetypes spy leader creator.
  • Establishes the pre-china chapter geography: vietnam, india, jordan, israel, egypt, peru. China was not the start of his traveling life — it was the longest stay.
  • Every entry is a near-miss or a stranger-encounter. Useful evidence for the fitfo pattern named explicitly later in the China chapter.
  • The supplement stack tail (magnesium, fish oils, D3, BCAA, creatine) is the only non-narrative item — flagged as its own concept since it'll likely recur.

Open questions

  • Who's the "virgin 34 year old"? Worth a stub if a name surfaces.
  • Did any of these get written up long-form anywhere — in Google Docs, journal entries, the website? Worth grepping.
  • Are there China-specific entries he should add here? The list is curiously absent of Shanghai/Chengdu/Xi'an stories.

Wikilinks
